rawText:
When the Commission of Fine Arts approved a golden coin bearing Donald Trump's likeness -- fists clenched, brow furrowed, cheekbones chiselled, the word "liberty" inscribed above -- it was exploiting a loophole in federal law that bars living presidents from currency, a manoeuvre that broke with democratic tradition in a manner not seen since Calvin Coolidge appeared on a commemorative coin a century ago and that echoed, perhaps inadvertently, the practice of Julius Caesar, the first living Roman to put his face on money and a man whose coinage heralded the end of the republic.

The coin is merely the most visible symptom of a presidency that has turned aggressively inward, deploying symbols of personal power -- gigantic banners outside government buildings, a million-dollar gold card that places Trump's image larger than the Statue of Liberty -- alongside substantive policy shifts that are reshaping America's relationship with its own citizens and the wider world.

Nowhere is the inward turn more tangible than at the nation's airports, where Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials have been deployed to process travellers after the Department of Homeland Security shutdown, now in its second month, crippled the Transportation Security Administration's capacity -- a spectacle in which untrained, armed agents replace the civilian screeners whose union has demanded that its members "deserve to be paid, not replaced."

The administration's new cyber-strategy, a pugilistic seven-page document that pivots firmly from defence to offence, encapsulates the broader posture: rather than protecting domestic infrastructure from Iranian hackers who have already breached an American medical company and wiped data on more than 200,000 servers, the government is encouraging private firms to "hack back" -- a form of cyber-vigilante justice that remains technically illegal and that most corporate lawyers regard with deep wariness.

Yet the aggressive external posture sits uneasily alongside profound internal weakness, since the National Security Agency and Cyber Command went without a Senate-confirmed leader for nearly a year after a far-right influencer urged the president to fire the incumbent, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has been gutted over its role in election security, and the fundamental question of whether offensive strength can compensate for defensive vulnerability remains, as one former British cyber chief put it, a matter of "two quite different sports."

The fractures run deeper than institutional dysfunction, for a record number of Westerners -- roughly four million from thirty-one countries in 2024 alone, and perhaps three million from America in 2025 -- are fleeing their home countries, driven by a toxic combination of Robin Hood tax policies that push effective rates on the top one per cent toward historical highs, pandemic-era normalisation of geographical arbitrage, and a growing sense across all political persuasions that democracy itself is broken.

The emergence of what analysts call the "expat economy" represents a quiet but consequential rebuke to America's inward turn, as private-sector data reveal that for the first time in years more American tech workers are moving to Europe than vice versa, as the number of Americans living in Germany has surged by over sixty per cent in a decade, and as Trump's deportation efforts may provoke up to one million additional departures in 2026 -- a brain drain that forfeits future tax revenues and, according to research from Tel Aviv University, correlates convincingly with democratic decline.

Meanwhile, the unveiling of a Christopher Columbus statue outside a federal building adjacent to the White House -- a replica of one that protesters threw into Baltimore's harbour after the killing of George Floyd, complete with recovered pieces of the original -- served as a pointed symbol of the administration's determination to relitigate cultural battles rather than address the structural grievances that provoked the protests in the first place.

The Supreme Court's decision to strike down most of Trump's tariffs has further unravelled the administration's trade strategy, leaving countries that accepted one-sided concessions facing fresh American investigations and prompting analysts to predict that many will simply walk away from agreements that have, in the words of one Indian commentator, "lost much of their value."

What emerges from this tangle of coins and cyber-attacks, airport chaos and emigration surges, cultural provocation and legal setbacks, is a portrait of a superpower turning inward not from a position of confident self-sufficiency but from one of deepening fragmentation -- a nation that stamps its leader's face on gold while its airports seize up, that tells firms to hack back while its own defences crumble, and that watches its most talented citizens depart even as it erects ever higher walls against the world.

overview.title: America's Inward Turn: Power, Symbols, and Fractures

sentences.original: When the Commission of Fine Arts approved a golden coin bearing Donald Trump's likeness -- fists clenched, brow furrowed, cheekbones chiselled, the word "liberty" inscribed above -- it was exploiting a loophole in federal law that bars living presidents from currency, a manoeuvre that broke with democratic tradition in a manner not seen since Calvin Coolidge appeared on a commemorative coin a century ago and that echoed, perhaps inadvertently, the practice of Julius Caesar, the first living Roman to put his face on money and a man whose coinage heralded the end of the republic.
sentences.translation: 미술위원회가 도널드 트럼프의 초상이 새겨진 금화를 승인했을 때 -- 주먹을 쥐고, 미간을 찌푸리고, 광대뼈가 조각된 모습 위에 '자유'라는 단어가 새겨진 -- 이는 현직 대통령의 화폐 등장을 금지하는 연방법의 허점을 이용한 것이었으며, 1세기 전 캘빈 쿨리지가 기념 주화에 등장한 이후 볼 수 없었던 방식으로 민주적 전통을 깨뜨린 행위이자, 아마도 의도치 않게 율리우스 카이사르 -- 살아 있는 로마인 최초로 자신의 얼굴을 화폐에 새긴 인물이자 그의 주화가 공화국의 종말을 예고한 인물 -- 의 관행을 되풀이한 것이었다.

grammarElements.text: When the Commission of Fine Arts approved a golden coin bearing ...
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 + 현재분사 수식

grammarElements.text: it was exploiting a loophole in federal law
grammarElements.label: 주절 (과거진행)

grammarElements.text: that bars living presidents from currency
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (법의 내용)

grammarElements.text: a manoeuvre that broke with ... and that echoed ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 이중 관계절

vocabulary.example: Tax lawyers are skilled at finding loopholes in the legislation.
The mint issued a commemorative coin to mark the centenary.

expressions.usage: 오래된 관례나 규범을 의도적으로 어겼을 때 사용하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Action] was exploiting a loophole in [law] that [prohibition], a manoeuvre that [historical comparison]
patterns.explanation: 법적 허점 이용을 역사적 전례와 연결시키는 패턴
examples: The ruling was exploiting a loophole in trade law that exempts digital services, a manoeuvre that recalled the regulatory arbitrage of the early internet era.
The exemption was exploiting a loophole in emissions law that excludes older vehicles, a manoeuvre that undermined the spirit of the regulation.

drillQuestions.question: It was exploiting a ______ in federal law that bars living presidents from currency.

sentences.notes: 율리우스 카이사르와의 비교가 단순한 주화 문제를 공화정의 위기로 격상시키며, 독자에게 역사적 경고를 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a golden coin bearing Donald Trump's likeness
exploiting a loophole in federal law
broke with democratic tradition
whose coinage heralded the end of the republic

sentences.original: The coin is merely the most visible symptom of a presidency that has turned aggressively inward, deploying symbols of personal power -- gigantic banners outside government buildings, a million-dollar gold card that places Trump's image larger than the Statue of Liberty -- alongside substantive policy shifts that are reshaping America's relationship with its own citizens and the wider world.
sentences.translation: 이 주화는 공격적으로 내향화된 대통령 권력의 가장 눈에 띄는 징후일 뿐이며, 개인 권력의 상징물 -- 정부 건물 앞의 거대한 현수막, 트럼프의 이미지가 자유의 여신상보다 더 크게 들어간 100만 달러짜리 금 카드 -- 을 미국과 자국 시민 및 외부 세계와의 관계를 재편하는 실질적 정책 전환과 함께 배치하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The coin is merely the most visible symptom
grammarElements.label: 주어 + be동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: of a presidency that has turned aggressively inward
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: deploying symbols of personal power
grammarElements.label: 분사구문

grammarElements.text: alongside substantive policy shifts that are reshaping ...
grammarElements.label: alongside + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The agreement included substantive reforms, not just cosmetic changes.

expressions.usage: 표면적 현상이 더 깊은 문제의 일부에 불과하다는 것을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Phenomenon] is merely the most visible symptom of [deeper trend]
patterns.explanation: 눈에 보이는 사건이 더 광범위한 변화의 일부임을 시사하는 패턴
examples: The layoffs are merely the most visible symptom of a business model in decline.
The protest is merely the most visible symptom of a society divided by inequality.

drillQuestions.question: The coin is merely the most visible ______ of a presidency that has turned aggressively inward.

sentences.notes: merely라는 부사가 주화를 빙산의 일각으로 격하시키면서, 더 깊은 구조적 변화에 주목하게 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: merely the most visible symptom
a presidency that has turned aggressively inward
symbols of personal power
substantive policy shifts

sentences.original: Nowhere is the inward turn more tangible than at the nation's airports, where Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials have been deployed to process travellers after the Department of Homeland Security shutdown, now in its second month, crippled the Transportation Security Administration's capacity -- a spectacle in which untrained, armed agents replace the civilian screeners whose union has demanded that its members "deserve to be paid, not replaced."
sentences.translation: 이 내향적 전환이 가장 실감나는 곳은 전국의 공항이다. 국토안보부의 업무 중단이 2개월째에 접어들면서 교통안전청의 역량을 마비시킨 후, 이민세관단속국 직원들이 여행객 처리에 투입되었다 -- 훈련받지 않은 무장 요원이 노조가 조합원들은 "대체되는 것이 아니라 급여를 받을 자격이 있다"고 요구해 온 민간 검색요원을 대체하는 광경이다.

grammarElements.text: Nowhere is the inward turn more tangible than at ...
grammarElements.label: 도치 구문 (Nowhere + be동사 + 주어)

grammarElements.text: where Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials have been deployed
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (장소)

grammarElements.text: after the Department of Homeland Security shutdown ... crippled ...
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: a spectacle in which untrained, armed agents replace ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명

vocabulary.example: The effects of the policy shift became tangible within weeks.

expressions.usage: 특정 장소나 사례에서 현상이 가장 극명하게 나타남을 강조하는 도치 표현

patterns.pattern: Nowhere is [trend] more [adjective] than at [location], where [specific evidence]
patterns.explanation: 추상적 추세가 특정 장소에서 가장 극명하게 드러남을 보여주는 도치 패턴
examples: Nowhere is the housing crisis more acute than in the capital, where a studio apartment costs more than a family home elsewhere.
Nowhere is the skills gap more evident than on the factory floor, where machines sit idle for want of qualified operators.

drillQuestions.question: Nowhere is the inward turn more ______ than at the nation's airports.

sentences.notes: Nowhere is ... more ... than 도치 구문이 공항의 혼란을 미국 내향화의 가장 극명한 현장으로 부각시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: Nowhere is the inward turn more tangible
Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials
crippled the Transportation Security Administration's capacity
untrained, armed agents replace the civilian screeners
koreanAlignment.ko: 훈련받지 않은 무장 요원이 민간 검색요원을 대체하는

sentences.original: The administration's new cyber-strategy, a pugilistic seven-page document that pivots firmly from defence to offence, encapsulates the broader posture: rather than protecting domestic infrastructure from Iranian hackers who have already breached an American medical company and wiped data on more than 200,000 servers, the government is encouraging private firms to "hack back" -- a form of cyber-vigilante justice that remains technically illegal and that most corporate lawyers regard with deep wariness.
sentences.translation: 행정부의 새 사이버 전략은 방어에서 공격으로 확고히 전환하는 호전적인 7페이지짜리 문서로, 보다 광범위한 기조를 압축한다: 이미 미국 의료 회사를 뚫고 20만 대 이상의 서버 데이터를 삭제한 이란 해커로부터 국내 인프라를 보호하는 대신, 정부는 민간 기업에 '역해킹'을 권장하고 있다 -- 기술적으로 여전히 불법이며 대부분의 기업 법무팀이 깊은 경계심으로 바라보는 일종의 사이버 자경단 정의이다.

grammarElements.text: The administration's new cyber-strategy, a pugilistic seven-page document
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격

grammarElements.text: encapsulates the broader posture
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: rather than protecting ..., the government is encouraging ...
grammarElements.label: rather than 대조 구문

grammarElements.text: a form of cyber-vigilante justice that remains ... and that most corporate lawyers regard ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 이중 관계절

vocabulary.example: The memo adopted a pugilistic tone that alarmed the diplomatic community.

expressions.usage: 특정 정책이나 제안에 대한 전문가들의 신중한 우려를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Strategy] encapsulates the broader posture: rather than [defensive action], [subject] is [offensive action]
patterns.explanation: 특정 전략이 전체 기조를 요약함을 보여주고, 방어와 공격의 대비를 제시하는 패턴
examples: The new trade policy encapsulates the broader posture: rather than negotiating multilateral deals, the administration is imposing unilateral conditions.
The hiring freeze encapsulates the broader posture: rather than investing in growth, the board is hoarding cash.

drillQuestions.question: The administration's new cyber-strategy ______ the broader posture.

sentences.notes: pugilistic이라는 권투 은유가 사이버 전략의 공격성을 강렬하게 전달하며, hack back의 법적 모순이 정책의 위험성을 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a pugilistic seven-page document
pivots firmly from defence to offence
hack back
cyber-vigilante justice

sentences.original: Yet the aggressive external posture sits uneasily alongside profound internal weakness, since the National Security Agency and Cyber Command went without a Senate-confirmed leader for nearly a year after a far-right influencer urged the president to fire the incumbent, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has been gutted over its role in election security, and the fundamental question of whether offensive strength can compensate for defensive vulnerability remains, as one former British cyber chief put it, a matter of "two quite different sports."
sentences.translation: 그러나 공격적인 외부 기조는 심각한 내부 약점과 불편하게 공존한다. 국가안보국과 사이버사령부는 극우 인플루언서가 대통령에게 현직자 해임을 촉구한 후 거의 1년간 상원 인준 지도자 없이 운영되었고, 사이버보안기반시설보안청은 선거 보안 역할 때문에 무력화되었으며, 공격력이 방어 취약성을 보상할 수 있는지에 대한 근본적인 질문은 한 전직 영국 사이버 수장의 말처럼 "전혀 다른 두 종목"의 문제로 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: the aggressive external posture sits uneasily alongside profound internal weakness
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 부사 + 전치사구)

grammarElements.text: since the NSA and Cyber Command went without ... , the CISA has been gutted ... , and the fundamental question ... remains ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 since절 (이유)

grammarElements.text: as one former British cyber chief put it
grammarElements.label: 삽입절 (인용 출처)

vocabulary.example: Budget cuts have gutted the agency's ability to enforce regulations.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 모순된 현실이 공존하는 긴장 관계를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Aggressive posture] sits uneasily alongside [internal weakness], since [evidence 1], [evidence 2], and [evidence 3]
patterns.explanation: 외부 강경 기조와 내부 약점의 모순을 대비시키고 증거를 나열하는 패턴
examples: The firm's bold expansion plan sits uneasily alongside its shrinking cash reserves, since debts have doubled, key staff have departed, and the board has delayed the audit.
The nation's military buildup sits uneasily alongside its eroding social fabric, since schools are underfunded, hospitals are closing, and trust in institutions has collapsed.

drillQuestions.question: The aggressive external posture sits ______ alongside profound internal weakness.

sentences.notes: two quite different sports라는 인용이 공격과 방어의 근본적 차이를 스포츠 비유로 선명하게 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: sits uneasily alongside profound internal weakness
went without a Senate-confirmed leader
has been gutted over its role in election security
two quite different sports

sentences.original: The fractures run deeper than institutional dysfunction, for a record number of Westerners -- roughly four million from thirty-one countries in 2024 alone, and perhaps three million from America in 2025 -- are fleeing their home countries, driven by a toxic combination of Robin Hood tax policies that push effective rates on the top one per cent toward historical highs, pandemic-era normalisation of geographical arbitrage, and a growing sense across all political persuasions that democracy itself is broken.
sentences.translation: 균열은 제도적 기능 장애보다 더 깊이 뻗어 있다. 기록적 수의 서구인들이 -- 2024년에만 31개국에서 약 400만 명, 2025년에는 미국에서 300만 명에 이를 것으로 추산되는 -- 자국을 떠나고 있으며, 그 동인은 상위 1퍼센트의 실효세율을 역사적 고점으로 밀어올리는 로빈후드식 세금 정책, 팬데믹 시기에 정상화된 지리적 차익거래, 그리고 모든 정치적 성향에 걸쳐 민주주의 자체가 망가졌다는 인식이 확산되는 독성 조합이다.

grammarElements.text: The fractures run deeper than institutional dysfunction
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 비교급)

grammarElements.text: for a record number of Westerners are fleeing their home countries
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (for)

grammarElements.text: driven by a toxic combination of ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식어

grammarElements.text: Robin Hood tax policies ..., normalisation ..., and a growing sense ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 나열 (원인)

vocabulary.meaning: 차익거래, 가격 차이를 이용한 이익 추구
vocabulary.example: Remote work has enabled a new form of geographical arbitrage.

expressions.usage: 여러 부정적 요인이 결합하여 더 나쁜 결과를 만들 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The [problems] run deeper than [surface issue], for [evidence of deeper cause]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 문제 아래 더 근본적인 원인이 있음을 밝히는 패턴
examples: The complaints run deeper than poor service, for customers have lost trust in the brand itself.
The divisions run deeper than policy disagreement, for voters no longer share a common set of facts.

drillQuestions.question: The fractures run ______ than institutional dysfunction.

sentences.notes: Robin Hood tax policies라는 명명이 재분배 정책을 비유적으로 포착하며, 이민 데이터가 '발로 하는 투표'의 규모를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The fractures run deeper than institutional dysfunction
koreanAlignment.ko: 균열은 제도적 기능 장애보다 더 깊이 뻗어 있다

koreanAlignment.en: a toxic combination of
geographical arbitrage
democracy itself is broken

pronunciationNotes: arbitrage: /ˈɑːr.bɪ.trɑːʒ/ 첫 음절에 강세, 마지막은 프랑스식 /ʒ/

sentences.original: The emergence of what analysts call the "expat economy" represents a quiet but consequential rebuke to America's inward turn, as private-sector data reveal that for the first time in years more American tech workers are moving to Europe than vice versa, as the number of Americans living in Germany has surged by over sixty per cent in a decade, and as Trump's deportation efforts may provoke up to one million additional departures in 2026 -- a brain drain that forfeits future tax revenues and, according to research from Tel Aviv University, correlates convincingly with democratic decline.
sentences.translation: 분석가들이 '해외이주 경제'라 부르는 현상의 등장은 미국의 내향 전환에 대한 조용하지만 중대한 반발이다. 민간 부문 데이터에 따르면 수년 만에 처음으로 유럽으로 이주하는 미국 기술 인력이 그 반대보다 더 많아졌고, 독일에 거주하는 미국인의 수는 10년간 60퍼센트 이상 급증했으며, 트럼프의 추방 정책은 2026년에 최대 100만 명의 추가 이탈을 촉발할 수 있다 -- 미래 세수를 포기하게 하고, 텔아비브대학교의 연구에 따르면 민주주의 쇠퇴와 설득력 있게 상관관계를 보이는 두뇌 유출이다.

grammarElements.text: The emergence of what analysts call the 'expat economy'
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: represents a quiet but consequential rebuke
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: as ... reveal ..., as ... has surged ..., and as ... may provoke ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 as절 (근거)

grammarElements.text: a brain drain that forfeits ... and correlates ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 이중 관계절

vocabulary.example: The election result was a sharp rebuke to the incumbent government.

expressions.usage: 고급 인력 이탈이 장기적으로 가져오는 경제적 손실을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The emergence of [phenomenon] represents a [quiet/sharp] rebuke to [policy], as [data 1], as [data 2], and as [data 3]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 현상이 기존 정책에 대한 반발임을 삼중 데이터로 입증하는 패턴
examples: The rise of remote work represents a quiet rebuke to return-to-office mandates, as vacancy rates climb, as productivity stays flat, and as top performers leave for flexible competitors.
The growth of local currencies represents a grassroots rebuke to central bank policy, as adoption spreads, as transaction volumes rise, and as small businesses report increased stability.

drillQuestions.question: The expat economy represents a quiet but consequential ______ to America's inward turn.

sentences.notes: quiet but consequential이라는 형용사 쌍이 소리 없는 이민의 파괴력을 정확히 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the 'expat economy'
a quiet but consequential rebuke
more American tech workers are moving to Europe than vice versa
koreanAlignment.ko: 유럽으로 이주하는 미국 기술 인력이 그 반대보다 더 많아졌고

koreanAlignment.en: a brain drain that forfeits future tax revenues
koreanAlignment.ko: 미래 세수를 포기하게 하는 두뇌 유출

sentences.original: Meanwhile, the unveiling of a Christopher Columbus statue outside a federal building adjacent to the White House -- a replica of one that protesters threw into Baltimore's harbour after the killing of George Floyd, complete with recovered pieces of the original -- served as a pointed symbol of the administration's determination to relitigate cultural battles rather than address the structural grievances that provoked the protests in the first place.
sentences.translation: 한편, 백악관 인접 연방 건물 앞에 크리스토퍼 콜럼버스 동상을 공개한 것은 -- 조지 플로이드 사망 후 시위대가 볼티모어 항구에 던져 넣은 동상의 복제품으로, 원본의 회수된 조각까지 포함된 -- 행정부가 애초에 시위를 촉발한 구조적 불만을 해결하기보다 문화 전쟁을 재심리하겠다는 결의를 노골적으로 드러낸 상징이었다.

grammarElements.text: the unveiling of a Christopher Columbus statue
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: served as a pointed symbol
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: a replica of one that protesters threw into Baltimore's harbour
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: to relitigate cultural battles rather than address the structural grievances
grammarElements.label: to부정사 + rather than 대조

vocabulary.meaning: 재심리하다, 이미 결정된 문제를 다시 다투다
vocabulary.example: The party spent more time relitigating past defeats than planning future campaigns.

expressions.usage: 행위가 의도적으로 특정 메시지를 전달하려 한다는 것을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Action] served as a pointed symbol of [determination] to [provocative choice] rather than [constructive alternative]
patterns.explanation: 상징적 행위가 생산적 대안 대신 도발적 선택을 했음을 비판하는 패턴
examples: The funding cut served as a pointed symbol of the board's determination to punish dissent rather than fix the underlying management failures.
The border ceremony served as a pointed symbol of the government's determination to project strength rather than negotiate a lasting peace.

drillQuestions.question: The unveiling served as a pointed symbol of the administration's determination to ______ cultural battles.

sentences.notes: 원본 동상의 파편을 복제품에 포함시킨 디테일이 문화 전쟁의 의도적 도발성을 극대화하는 수사적 장치이다.

koreanAlignment.en: the unveiling of a Christopher Columbus statue
a pointed symbol
relitigate cultural battles
the structural grievances that provoked the protests

sentences.original: The Supreme Court's decision to strike down most of Trump's tariffs has further unravelled the administration's trade strategy, leaving countries that accepted one-sided concessions facing fresh American investigations and prompting analysts to predict that many will simply walk away from agreements that have, in the words of one Indian commentator, "lost much of their value."
sentences.translation: 대법원이 트럼프의 관세 대부분을 무효화하기로 한 결정은 행정부의 무역 전략을 더욱 해체시켰고, 일방적 양보를 수용한 국가들은 새로운 미국 조사에 직면하게 되었으며, 분석가들은 많은 국가가 한 인도 논평가의 표현대로 "가치를 대부분 잃은" 협정에서 그냥 발을 뺄 것이라고 예측하게 되었다.

grammarElements.text: The Supreme Court's decision to strike down most of Trump's tariffs
grammarElements.label: 주어 + to부정사 수식

grammarElements.text: has further unravelled
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: the administration's trade strategy
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: leaving ... and prompting ...
grammarElements.label: 이중 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The alliance began to unravel after the trade dispute escalated.

expressions.usage: 협상이나 협정에서 더 이상 참여하지 않기로 결정할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Decision] has further unravelled [strategy], leaving [affected parties] facing [consequence] and prompting [observers] to predict [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 결정이 연쇄적으로 전략 붕괴, 피해자 발생, 예측을 촉발하는 패턴
examples: The ruling has further unravelled the merger plan, leaving shareholders facing losses and prompting regulators to investigate related deals.
The recall has further unravelled the product launch, leaving retailers facing empty shelves and prompting competitors to accelerate their own releases.

drillQuestions.question: The Supreme Court's decision has further ______ the administration's trade strategy.

sentences.notes: 이중 분사구문(leaving ... and prompting ...)이 대법원 판결의 이중 파급효과를 효율적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: strike down most of Trump's tariffs
has further unravelled the administration's trade strategy
one-sided concessions
walk away from agreements

sentences.original: What emerges from this tangle of coins and cyber-attacks, airport chaos and emigration surges, cultural provocation and legal setbacks, is a portrait of a superpower turning inward not from a position of confident self-sufficiency but from one of deepening fragmentation -- a nation that stamps its leader's face on gold while its airports seize up, that tells firms to hack back while its own defences crumble, and that watches its most talented citizens depart even as it erects ever higher walls against the world.
sentences.translation: 주화와 사이버 공격, 공항의 혼란과 이민 급증, 문화적 도발과 법적 좌절이라는 이 뒤엉킨 실타래에서 떠오르는 것은, 자신만만한 자급자족의 위치가 아니라 심화하는 분열의 위치에서 내향으로 돌아서는 초강대국의 초상이다 -- 공항이 마비되는 와중에 지도자의 얼굴을 금화에 찍는 나라, 자국 방어가 무너지면서 기업에 역해킹을 지시하는 나라, 세계를 향해 점점 높은 벽을 세우면서 가장 재능 있는 시민들이 떠나는 것을 지켜보는 나라의 초상이다.

grammarElements.text: What emerges from this tangle of ...
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: a portrait of a superpower turning inward not from ... but from ...
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: a nation that stamps ..., that tells ..., and that watches ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 that절 동격

vocabulary.example: Political fragmentation has made coalition-building nearly impossible.
The investigation revealed a tangle of conflicting interests and hidden debts.

expressions.phrase: erects ever higher walls against the world
expressions.meaning: 세계를 향해 점점 더 높은 벽을 세우다
expressions.usage: 고립주의적 정책이 심화되는 것을 은유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: What emerges from this tangle of [contrasts] is a portrait of [subject] -- a [entity] that [irony 1], that [irony 2], and that [irony 3]
patterns.explanation: 여러 모순적 소재를 하나의 초상으로 종합하고, 삼중 역설로 마무리하는 결론 패턴
examples: What emerges from this tangle of ambition and failure is a portrait of a company that promised innovation, that delivered mediocrity, and that blamed its customers for both.
What emerges from this tangle of reform and resistance is a portrait of a society that demands change, that fears its consequences, and that punishes the leaders who attempt it.

drillQuestions.question: What emerges is a portrait of a superpower turning inward not from confident self-sufficiency but from deepening ______.

sentences.notes: 삼중 that절의 각 항이 모두 '~하면서 동시에 ~하는' 역설 구조를 반복하며, 글 전체의 모순을 압축적으로 종합한다.

koreanAlignment.en: this tangle of coins and cyber-attacks
not from confident self-sufficiency but from deepening fragmentation
stamps its leader's face on gold while its airports seize up
koreanAlignment.ko: 공항이 마비되는 와중에 지도자의 얼굴을 금화에 찍는

koreanAlignment.en: erects ever higher walls against the world
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계를 향해 점점 높은 벽을 세우면서
